title: Retrieval Review: Resilient Form Interface Design
summary: Retrieval Review: Resilient Form Interface Design retrieves prior skills in an original changed browser state and input-mode constraints scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css form inheritance, css form layout, css form focus, css form errors, css form native custom, css form autofill motion in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real changed browser state and input-mode constraints artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Community Needs Survey form interface here. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Continue the resilient form design system here. */

options.text: Changed browser state and input-mode constraints case 1: form controls inherit every font and color property automatically.
Changed browser state and input-mode constraints case 1: inspect native computed styles, normalize only shared design-system properties, and retest every control type and state across browsers.
Changed browser state and input-mode constraints case 1: apply appearance none to every input select and button before deciding how each control works.

options.text: Changed browser state and input-mode constraints case 3: removing outlines is safe when hover styling exists.

options.text: Changed browser state and input-mode constraints case 6: autofill colors can be overridden without checking readability or user expectations.
